response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings over 20 years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems, with demonstrated Staff-level ownership, polyglot capability, and credible distributed-systems work at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second. However, the Principal Growth/Experimentation Systems role is fundamentally about owning experimentation platforms, telemetry infrastructure, feature-flagging systems, metrics pipelines, and statistical analysis workflows — none of which surface in this resume. The candidate's career arc is full-stack product engineering and open-source tooling, not growth engineering or measurement platform architecture, and the absence of any experimentation, A/B testing, or observability work is a critical gap for an IC6 hire.
strengths: 20+ years of engineering across diverse domains with Staff-level ownership and polyglot proficiency including C/C++, TypeScript, Java, and D
Proven distributed-systems experience at Sociomantic processing millions of HTTP requests/second using low-latency async microservices
Recent AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling work at re:cinq, including MCP frameworks, which aligns with the AI-first tooling context
concerns: No evidence of experimentation platform work: zero mention of A/B testing, feature flagging, experiment assignment, rollout automation, or bias detection
No telemetry, metrics, or observability infrastructure ownership despite these being core pillars of the role's required qualifications
Demonstrated scope and title (Senior/Staff) fall short of the cross-team, multi-workstream Principal/IC6 leadership and stakeholder influence the role requires

key_factors.factor: No experimentation or growth systems experience
No telemetry or metrics infrastructure ownership
Distributed systems depth and long engineering track record
